projects.title: Portfolio
projects.description: This is My portfolio website which is created my me  to demonstrate my react skills

blogs.title: Emmet for HTML: A Beginner’s Guide to Writing Faster Markup
blogs.excerpt: Writing HTML without Emmet feels slow and tiring, especially for beginners. You have to type every opening tag, closing tag, and indentation manually, which takes time and increases the chance of mistakes.

blogs.title: Understanding HTML Tags and Elements
blogs.excerpt: HTML (HyperText Markup Language) is the basic building block of the web. It is used to create the structure of a webpage. HTML tells the browser what content exists on a page, like headings, paragraphs, images, links, forms, and buttons.

blogs.title: How a Browser Works: A Beginner-Friendly Guide to Browser Internals
blogs.excerpt: A browser is not just something that opens websites; it is actually a translator, loader, and security guard between me and the internet. When I type a website name, the browser first figures out where that site lives using DNS, then sends requests to the server using internet rules like HTTP or HTTPS.

blogs.title: TCP Working: 3-Way Handshake & Reliable Communication
blogs.excerpt: Imagine sending a long message over the internet without any rules. Some parts of the message may arrive late, some may arrive twice, and some parts may not arrive at all. The message may also reach in the wrong order. Because of this, the receiver will not know which part is correct, which part is missing, or how to put the message back together properly.

blogs.title: TCP vs UDP
blogs.excerpt: A protocol is a set of rules for communication; it tells computers how to talk, what format to use, and when to send and receive data. TCP(Transmission Control Protocol) and UDP(User Datagram Protocol) is a protocol by which we transmit data

blogs.title: Understanding Network Devices
blogs.excerpt: Modem stands for Modulator/Demodulator. A modem is a networking device that is used to connect our computer (or devices ) to the internet. The main work of a modem is to make communication between analog signal and a digital signal. It acts like a bridge between you and your internet service provider

blogs.title: How DNS Resolution Works
blogs.excerpt: The Domain Name System (DNS) is like the phonebook of the internet. It helps us use easy-to-remember website names (like google.com) and converts them into IP addresses that computers understand. Instead of having one central database, DNS is distributed and hierarchical, which means different servers are responsible for different parts of the internet.

blogs.title: Getting Started with cURL
blogs.excerpt: cURL is a very basic and powerful tool that programmers use to communicate directly with a server. The word cURL stands for Client URL, and it is a command-line tool, which means you use it from the terminal instead of a browser.

blogs.title: CSS Selectors 101: Targeting Elements with Precision
blogs.excerpt: CSS selectors are used to choose which HTML elements we want to style on a web page. They help us tell the browser exactly where to apply styles like color, font, spacing, and layout. Instead of styling everything, selectors let us target only specific elements or a group of elements. The HTML element that gets selected by a CSS selector is called the subject of the selector.

blogs.title: DNS Record Types Explained
blogs.excerpt:
How does a browser know where a website lives?
When you type a website URL into your browser, the browser doesn’t immediately know where that website lives. First, it needs to find the physical server (machine) where the website is hosted.
